items.title: Nordlys Labs
items.description: Intelligent LLM routing system that selects the best model for each task. Ranked #1 on SWE-bench, defeating frontier models, and scaled to 500+ users.
items.longDescription:
## About

Nordlys Labs is an intelligent LLM routing startup that selects the best model for each task. We were selected by the Antler accelerator and scaled to over 500 users.

## Highlights

- Ranked **#1 on SWE-bench**, defeating frontier models
- Scaled to **500+ active users**
- Selected by the **Antler** accelerator program
- Production stack: Python, FastAPI, LangChain, Redis, PostgreSQL, Docker

items.title: ZeroToDiffusion
items.description: From-scratch implementation of diffusion models: derived the probability theory, built a VAE, then implemented DDPM step by step to understand image generation from first principles.
items.longDescription:
## About

I kept reading about Stable Diffusion and DALL-E but the math never clicked from blog posts alone. So I decided to go through it properly. Pen and paper first, then code.

## How I went about it

Week 1 was just probability: Gaussians, Bayes, KL divergence, ELBO. Boring but necessary. Turns out the entire loss function makes sense once you actually derive it yourself.

Then I built a VAE on MNIST. VAEs and diffusion models share the same probabilistic backbone, so this made the transition way smoother than jumping straight into DDPM.

After that: the forward process (gradually adding noise to images), the reverse process (learning to denoise), training a UNet to predict the noise, and finally generating images from pure noise. Seeing it work for the first time was honestly pretty satisfying.

## Takeaways

- The connection between VAEs and diffusion clicked once I saw both as learning to reverse a destruction process
- Noise schedules matter more than I expected for sample quality
- Attention layers are what give diffusion models their ability to capture global structure

## Papers I worked through

- Kingma & Welling (2014), VAEs
- Ho et al. (2020), DDPM

items.title: KV Cache Optimization
items.description: Research on KV-cache compression for LLMs. Starts from a comparison of attention mechanisms (MHA / MQA / GQA / MLA) on toy models, then develops production-scale techniques — streaming recurrent state conversion and multi-tier adaptive compression — targeting Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct.
items.longDescription:
## About

Research project on KV cache compression for large language models. The work starts from a comparison of attention mechanisms (MHA, MQA, GQA, MLA) on small toy transformers, then scales up to production techniques targeting Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct.

## Approach

**AttentionHeads** — Foundational comparison of four attention architectures on TinyStories and SimpleStories, each implemented as a ~16M-parameter decoder-only transformer to make the perplexity-vs-cache trade-off easy to inspect.

**Streaming Attention** — Production-scale optimization for Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct. Streaming heads (identified using DuoAttention) are converted to fixed-size recurrent state matrices, while retrieval heads keep full or tiered KV cache. A two-stage calibration pipeline aligns decay parameters per-head and then fine-tunes with LoRA. Multi-tier adaptive compression mixes FP16, INT8, INT4 and outright eviction so each token sits at the right precision.

**KV Bench** — A unified benchmarking framework comparing 8 KV cache strategies: FullKV baseline, H2O, SnapKV, INT8 / INT4 uniform quantization, Stratigraphic compression, StreamingAttention, and a Hybrid configuration.

## Key Results

INT8 matches the FullKV baseline on perplexity (7.97 vs 7.96) at half the memory. Stratigraphic compression reaches 38% memory with only a small perplexity bump (8.05). At 50% retention, SnapKV holds 8.15 and H2O 8.31, both noticeably above the INT8 line. INT4 lands at 25% memory and 8.42 perplexity — the cheapest configuration tested but the most lossy.

## References

Vaswani et al. 2017 (Attention Is All You Need), Shazeer 2019 (MQA), Ainslie et al. 2023 (GQA), Xiao et al. 2024 (DuoAttention), Zhang et al. 2023 (H2O), Li et al. 2024 (SnapKV).

items.title: Transformer From Stones
items.description: From-scratch implementation of the original Transformer architecture from 'Attention is All You Need', building multi-head attention, positional encoding, and training pipelines.
items.longDescription:
## About

A from-scratch implementation of the original Transformer architecture as described in the "Attention is All You Need" paper. Every component is built from first principles.

## Implementation Details

- Multi-head self-attention with scaled dot-product attention
- Sinusoidal positional encoding
- Layer normalization and residual connections
- Feed-forward networks with GELU activation
- Byte-pair encoding tokenization
- Training pipeline with gradient accumulation

items.title: Cyber Attack Detection
items.description: Machine learning-based threat detection system that identifies and classifies cyber attacks using network traffic analysis.
items.longDescription:
## About

A machine learning-based threat detection system that identifies and classifies cyber attacks by analyzing network traffic patterns.

## Approach

- Feature engineering on network traffic data
- Multiple ML classifiers (Random Forest, SVM, Neural Networks)
- Real-time classification pipeline
- High accuracy on benchmark datasets

items.title: Mixture of Models Framework
items.description: Research paper on the Mixture of Models (MoM) framework for intelligent LLM routing and model selection strategies.
items.longDescription:
## About

The MoM (Mixture of Models) framework is a research contribution to intelligent LLM routing. It introduces a systematic approach to selecting the best model for each task, combining routing strategies with cost optimization.

## Contributions

- Framework for LLM model selection
- Cost-aware routing strategies
- Benchmarking across multiple model families
- Presented at an online meetup with community engagement

items.title: Git-RLMs
items.description: Framework that lets LLMs analyze git repositories through an iterative REPL loop, exploring code incrementally instead of loading entire codebases into context.
items.longDescription:
## About

Git-RLMs is a framework that enables LLMs to explore and analyze git repositories through an iterative REPL (Read-Eval-Print Loop). Instead of dumping an entire codebase into context, the model incrementally discovers relevant code using 12 built-in tools.

## How It Works

1. The LLM sends a tool command (e.g., `git_log`, `read_file`, `search_code`)
2. The REPL executes it against the repository
3. Results feed back to the LLM for the next exploration step
4. Sub-model delegation via `llm_query` for focused analysis

## Available Tools

`git_log`, `git_show`, `git_diff`, `search_code`, `read_file`, `list_files`, `get_file_at_commit`, `git_blame`, `git_branches`, `git_tags`, `llm_query`, `python_exec`

## Features

- CLI and Python API
- Supports OpenAI and Anthropic as LLM providers
- Cost tracking and trajectory logging for reproducibility
- Sandboxed Python execution environment

items.title: CrossGen
items.description: Cross-domain idea generator that solves problems by mapping structural relationships across unrelated fields using analogical reasoning, not surface-level keyword matching.
items.longDescription:
## About

CrossGen is a cross-domain idea generator that discovers solutions by mapping deep structural relationships across unrelated fields. Instead of asking "what field resembles mine," it identifies fundamental relational patterns that transcend surface features using Gentner's Structure-Mapping Theory.

## Pipeline

1. Decompose the problem into domain-neutral relations
2. Abstract through four parallel frameworks (SAPPhIRE, Biologize, WordTree, TRIZ)
3. Expand to 6-8 distant domains
4. Mine for structural analogies
5. Synthesize actionable solutions
6. Evaluate on novelty, feasibility, and depth

## Design choices

- Maps RELATIONS, not surface features
- Mandatory honesty: at least three specific weak spots per solution
- 40 universal principles and 42 curated domains across 8 categories
- Deterministic TRIZ lookups complemented by LLM reasoning to prevent hallucination
- CLI and web UI with live streaming output

items.title: Vantage
items.description: AI investment assessment tool that evaluates whether an AI project is worth building, with three-year cost projections and industry benchmarks.
items.longDescription:
## About

Vantage is a strategic AI investment assessment platform that compresses 40+ consultant hours into a 5-10 minute evaluation. It combines financial modeling, industry benchmarking, and AI analysis.

## What it does

- Feasibility scoring across technical complexity, data readiness, and risk
- Three-year financial projections with risk-adjusted returns
- Industry benchmarking grounded in 200+ real-world case studies
- Build-vs-buy analysis and implementation roadmaps

## Tech Stack

Uses Claude API with LangGraph for multi-step reasoning, Sentence Transformers for semantic search, and ChromaDB for vector-based case study retrieval.

items.title: Get Me Clients
items.description: AI-powered lead generation tool for pharmaceutical sales teams. Discovers medical professionals across Algeria's 58 wilayas using Google Maps API, with LLM-driven search refinement and quality scoring.
items.longDescription:
## About

An AI-powered lead generation tool designed for pharmaceutical sales teams. It discovers doctors and medical professionals across Algeria's 58 wilayas using the Google Maps Places API, then scores and deduplicates leads for export.

## Workflow

1. **Business context input**: Describe your product and target audience
2. **AI refinement**: DeepSeek generates clarifying questions to narrow the search
3. **Search matrix**: Combines wilayas with medical specialties for full coverage
4. **Lead deduplication**: Uses rapidfuzz fuzzy matching to merge duplicate entries
5. **Quality scoring**: AI-driven scoring of each lead's relevance
6. **Excel export**: Download clean, scored lead lists

## Tech Stack

- Streamlit wizard UI for step-by-step interaction
- Google Maps Places API for location-based discovery
- DeepSeek LLM for search refinement and scoring
- rapidfuzz for lead deduplication

items.title: MedVoice Assistant
items.description: Voice-enabled AI assistant for medical sales reps. Features push-to-talk, real-time transcription, and PubMed integration for citing studies during conversations.
items.longDescription:
## About

A voice-enabled AI assistant built for medical sales representatives. It combines push-to-talk voice interaction with PubMed literature search, so reps can cite real studies while talking to doctors.

## Features

- **Push-to-talk**: Hold spacebar to speak, with real-time audio visualization
- **Speech-to-text**: Live transcription displayed as the user speaks
- **PubMed search**: AI tool that cites relevant medical studies during conversations
- **Auto-restart**: Conversation automatically resumes after each response
- **Authentication**: NextAuth-based user login and session management
- **Mistral AI backend**: Powered by Mistral for fast, accurate medical responses

items.title: Claude with Ads
items.description: Ad-supported AI chat application offering free access to Claude-level AI, funded by contextual advertising via Thrad's network.
items.longDescription:
## About

An ad-supported AI chat application that gives users free access to Claude-level AI, funded by contextual advertisements. After each AI response, contextual ads from Thrad's ad network are displayed.

## Features

- Real-time message streaming
- Email-based authentication via verification codes
- Contextual ad integration with Thrad network
- Redis-based data persistence
- Full-stack deployment on Vercel

items.title: Altair
items.description: Interactive 3D data visualization platform that turns CSV datasets into particle clouds, controlled by hand gestures via webcam using MediaPipe.
items.longDescription:
## About

Altair is an interactive 3D data visualization platform that transforms CSV datasets into explorable particle clouds. Users interact with data using hand gestures tracked in real time via their webcam, with no external hardware required.

## Features

- **Three topology modes**: Centralized, decentralized, and distributed particle layouts
- **Gesture controls**: Open hand for repulsion, pinch to select, fist to collapse, point to highlight
- **10K+ particle support** via Three.js instanced meshes for smooth 60fps rendering
- **On-device hand tracking** using MediaPipe for privacy (no data leaves the browser)
- CSV upload and automatic column mapping to particle properties

items.title: Prompt Repetition Experiment
items.description: Research replication testing whether prompt repetition improves small language models' accuracy, achieving near-doubling on ARC-Challenge benchmarks.
items.longDescription:
## About

A research experiment investigating whether repeating prompts within a single request boosts accuracy on standardized benchmarks for small language models.

## Findings

- Tested 9 open-source models (0.5B to 9B parameters)
- Benchmarks: ARC-Challenge, OpenBookQA, GSM8K, HellaSwag, NameIndex
- ARC-Challenge accuracy nearly doubled from 22% to 44% with triple repetition (p=0.022)
- Works because the second copy of the prompt gets to attend to the first, giving the model a quasi-bidirectional pass

items.title: LSTM from Scratch
items.description: From-scratch implementation of LSTM networks, building every gate and cell state mechanism from first principles with backpropagation through time.
items.longDescription:
## About

A from-scratch implementation of LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory) networks, building every gate and cell state mechanism from first principles to understand recurrent neural network architectures.

## Implementation

- Forget, input, and output gates
- Cell state management
- Backpropagation through time
- Training on sequence data

items.title: Mini Compiler
items.description: Lexical analyzer and parser for Mini3ADR, a simple language with variables, arithmetic, and control flow, built with Lex/Flex for a compiler construction course.
items.longDescription:
## About

A lexical analyzer and parser for Mini3ADR, a simple programming language designed for a compiler construction course. The project covers the front-end stages of compilation.

## Language Features (Mini3ADR)

- Integer variable declarations
- Assignment statements
- Arithmetic operators (+, -, *, /)
- Relational operators (<, >, ==, !=)
- Control flow: `while` loops and `if`/`else` statements

## Implementation

- **Lexer**: Tokenization with line tracking using Lex/Flex
- **Symbol table**: Tracks declared variables and their types
- **Parser**: Planned integration with Yacc/Bison for syntax analysis

items.title: Harry Potter Text Generator
items.description: Character-level text generator using vanilla RNNs trained on Harry Potter, exploring sequence modeling and the vanishing gradient problem in recurrent architectures.
items.longDescription:
## About

A character-level text generation project using Recurrent Neural Networks trained on the Harry Potter corpus. The goal was to understand sequence modeling from first principles and observe where vanilla RNNs break down.

## Technical Approach

- Character-level tokenization with one-hot encoding
- Vanilla RNN cells with tanh activation and hidden state propagation
- Cross-entropy loss with temperature-controlled softmax sampling
- Backpropagation through time (BPTT) with gradient clipping

## What I learned

- Vanishing gradients make it difficult for vanilla RNNs to capture long-range dependencies, the model loses coherence beyond short phrases
- Without gating mechanisms (LSTM/GRU), the hidden state saturates and forgets earlier context
- Temperature tuning is critical: too low produces repetitive text, too high produces gibberish
- This project motivated my subsequent LSTM from Scratch implementation to address these limitations

items.degree: BSc Computer Science & Artificial Intelligence (First-Class Honours)
items.description:
Royal Holloway is a research-intensive college of the University of London, with a Department of Computer Science known for AI, information security (ISG), and theoretical computer science.

**Selected courses:**

- Quantum Computation
- Natural Language Processing
- Machine Learning
- Deep Learning
- Compilers
- Computational Finance
- Database Management
- Software Engineering
- Object-Oriented Programming
- Introduction to Security
- Team Project
- Multi-Dimensional Data Processing
- Symbolic AI

items.title: Co-Founder & ML Research Lead
items.description:
- Co-founded Nordlys Labs and led ML research on **LLM routing** — picking the best model per request based on task signals.
- Designed and shipped a routing system that **ranked #1 on SWE-bench**, beating frontier models such as Claude and GPT.
- Scaled the platform to **500+ users**; built the production stack on Python / FastAPI / LangChain with Redis + PostgreSQL behind a Dockerised deploy.
- Selected for the **Antler** accelerator program; presented the *MoM (Mixture of Models)* framework at an online meetup.

items.title: Product Data Intern
items.description:
- Worked in the Product Data team on sports analytics pipelines feeding broadcast and betting customers.
- Built and maintained Python data-processing workflows; ran exploratory analyses and quality checks on event-level match data.
- Collaborated with product managers and data scientists to translate analytics requirements into shippable jobs.

items.company: ISG Smart Card & IoT Security Centre
items.title: Machine Learning Researcher
items.description:
- Research on **adversarial robustness** of ML-based intrusion detection systems for autonomous vehicles.
- Studied evasion attacks against in-vehicle network IDS models (CAN-bus traffic) and explored defences: adversarial training and input sanitisation.
- Trained PyTorch classifiers on automotive intrusion datasets and benchmarked their behaviour under perturbations.

items.title: Teaching Assistant
items.description:
- Teaching Assistant for **Object-Oriented Programming** and **Software Engineering** (Year 2).
- Run weekly lab sessions, debug student code in Java, and mark coursework / exam scripts.
- Hold office hours focused on design patterns, testing practice and OO modelling.
